name: Alonzo
bio:
Physical Appearance: Redguard male. Wears light armor - a cuirass and boots of quality craftsmanship, often with a helmet, paired with simple lower class pants. Armed with a blunt weapon and carries a black bow that marks him as one of the Black Bow Bandits.
Race: Redguard
Personality Keywords: bandit, predatory, nocturnal
Role: Alonzo is the leader of the Black Bow Bandits, a small gang of highwaymen who prey on travelers in the wilderness south of Leyawiin. He commands two other bandits - Black Brugo and Roxy Aric - and leads them in their criminal activities along the roads between Border Watch and the Ayleid ruin of Telepe.
Territory and Base: The Black Bow Bandits operate in the wilderness roughly halfway between Border Watch and Telepe, an area that allows them to ambush travelers on the southern roads. This remote location serves as their daytime base where they can watch for potential victims while remaining hidden from the authorities in Leyawiin.
Daily Routine: Alonzo and his gang spend their days in the wilderness from 6am to 8pm, lying in wait for travelers to rob. At 8pm, they abandon their exposed position and travel to the Ayleid ruin of Telepe, reaching it around 9pm. They spend the entire night sheltered within the ancient ruins, presumably sleeping and staying out of sight. At 6am, they emerge from Telepe and make their way back to their wilderness hideout to resume their banditry.
Equipment: As befits a bandit leader, Alonzo is well-equipped with quality light armor and a sturdy blunt weapon for close combat. His black bow - the signature weapon that gives his gang their name - is always at hand. His gear reflects both the practical needs of a career criminal and the success he's had at his violent trade.
Personality - The Bandit Leader: Alonzo leads a small but organized band of criminals who have established a routine of preying on travelers during the day and retreating to the safety of ancient ruins at night. His choice of territory between Border Watch and Telepe suggests tactical thinking - remote enough to avoid regular patrols, but positioned along routes where travelers pass. The nightly retreat to Telepe indicates a degree of caution; rather than camp in the open where they might be vulnerable, they use the ruin as a secure base. He's made banditry his profession and has at least two others who follow his leadership.
author: Bethesda
